title: Marketing-sourced deals close faster this week
anchor: Current week wins average 38 days to close versus 52 days over the prior 11 weeks.
connect: Mid-market accounts represent 7 of 9 wins this week.
body: Current week wins average 38 days to close versus 52 days over the prior 11 weeks. Mid-market accounts represent 7 of 9 wins this week.
trace: Marketing-sourced deal velocity compressed significantly in the current week period versus trailing 11-week baseline.

title: Mid-market SQLs up 50% in April
anchor: Month-to-date mid-market SQLs reach 147 versus the Q1 monthly average of 98.
connect: The trailing quarter monthly range was 95 to 101.
body: Month-to-date mid-market SQLs reach 147 versus the Q1 monthly average of 98. The trailing quarter monthly range was 95 to 101.
trace: Significant uptick in mid-market SQL volume in April against consistent Q1 baseline.

title: ABM leads show higher acceptance rates
anchor: ABM MQLs convert to SDR-accepted at 61% versus 42% for non-ABM leads.
connect: April volume split: 70 ABM MQLs, 77 non-ABM MQLs.
body: ABM MQLs convert to SDR-accepted at 61% versus 42% for non-ABM leads. April volume split: 70 ABM MQLs, 77 non-ABM MQLs.
trace: Clear acceptance rate advantage for ABM-sourced MQLs over general funnel leads in April.

title: Paid social overtakes paid search in pipeline creation
anchor: Q2 paid social generated $620K in pipeline versus $380K from paid search.
connect: Q1 showed the reverse pattern: paid search $720K, paid social $410K.
body: Q2 paid social generated $620K in pipeline versus $380K from paid search. Q1 showed the reverse pattern: paid search $720K, paid social $410K.
trace: Channel mix shifted between Q1 and Q2 with paid social emerging as the stronger pipeline generator.

title: Branded search volume doubles over six weeks
anchor: Weekly branded search climbed from 1000 to 1921, gaining week-over-week for six consecutive periods.
body: Weekly branded search climbed from 1000 to 1921, gaining week-over-week for six consecutive periods. Cumulative growth reaches 92% through April 19.
trace: Sustained branded search acceleration with no down weeks in the six-week tracking window.

title: Atlas brand mentions lead Beacon Systems by 52%
anchor: April Atlas SaaS mentions reach 1840 versus 1210 for Beacon Systems.
connect: Analyst mentions increased to 9 in the last 14 days from 6 in the prior 30.
body: April Atlas SaaS mentions reach 1840 versus 1210 for Beacon Systems. Analyst mentions increased to 9 in the last 14 days from 6 in the prior 30.
trace: Atlas leading the primary competitor in brand mention volume with acceleration in analyst coverage.

title: Direct traffic crosses organic search for first time
anchor: April traffic share: direct 42%, organic search 37%.
body: April traffic share: direct 42%, organic search 37%. The crossover occurred on April 15.
trace: Direct traffic overtook organic search as the primary web channel on a sustained basis starting mid-April.

title: Enterprise win rate climbs to 31% in Q2
anchor: Q2 enterprise deals close at 31% versus the four-quarter average of 22%.
connect: Average deal size reached $187K versus $142K historically.
body: Q2 enterprise deals close at 31% versus the four-quarter average of 22%. Average deal size reached $187K versus $142K historically.
trace: Enterprise segment showing both improved close rates and higher average contract values in Q2.

title: Event pipeline velocity accelerates after SaaS Connect
anchor: Q1 event-sourced wins close at 46 days versus 72 days in Q4.
connect: SaaS Connect contributed 12 of 17 event-sourced wins this quarter.
body: Q1 event-sourced wins close at 46 days versus 72 days in Q4. SaaS Connect contributed 12 of 17 event-sourced wins this quarter.
trace: Event-sourced deal cycles compressed significantly in Q1 with SaaS Connect as the primary source.

title: Q2 paid spend runs $84K under plan
anchor: Digital ads actual spend sits at $256K versus $340K planned through April 23.
connect: Reallocation moves the gap to SaaS Connect and Signal Summit events.
body: Digital ads actual spend sits at $256K versus $340K planned through April 23. Reallocation moves the gap to SaaS Connect and Signal Summit events.
trace: Significant underspend in digital ads being redirected to high-performing event channels.

title: Atlas Insights leads reference opt-ins at 75%
anchor: Reference participation: Atlas Insights 75%, Atlas Workflow 70%, Atlas Connect 29%.
connect: Connect product shows the lowest customer advocacy willingness.
body: Reference participation: Atlas Insights 75%, Atlas Workflow 70%, Atlas Connect 29%. Connect product shows the lowest customer advocacy willingness.
trace: Clear hierarchy in reference willingness across the product portfolio with Connect lagging significantly.

title: FinTech accounts dominate recent mid-market wins
anchor: Last 60 days mid-market closed-wins: 8 of 12 from FinTech industry.
connect: All 12 winning accounts run Snowflake plus dbt infrastructure.
body: Last 60 days mid-market closed-wins: 8 of 12 from FinTech industry. All 12 winning accounts run Snowflake plus dbt infrastructure.
trace: Strong concentration in FinTech vertical with complete correlation to specific data stack architecture.

title: SDR capacity exceeds inbound volume this week
anchor: Current week SDR capacity sits at 210 versus 142 inbound leads.
connect: Trailing four weeks averaged capacity 206, volume 201.
body: Current week SDR capacity sits at 210 versus 142 inbound leads. Trailing four weeks averaged capacity 206, volume 201.
trace: SDR capacity-to-volume ratio improved significantly this week versus balanced trailing baseline.

title: Named accounts show high-intent engagement
anchor: Three target accounts logged 5+ high-intent events this week.
connect: Two of the three sit on the Named Accounts list of 50.
body: Three target accounts logged 5+ high-intent events this week. Two of the three sit on the Named Accounts list of 50.
trace: High-intent activity concentrated in target accounts with majority coming from the Named Accounts priority list.

title: Atlas Assist launch campaign ready with locked calendar
anchor: Next product launch Atlas Assist scheduled for May 8.
connect: Associated campaign status shows ready with calendar locking April 24.
body: Next product launch Atlas Assist scheduled for May 8. Associated campaign status shows ready with calendar locking April 24.
trace: Product launch and marketing campaign coordination on track with calendar finalization this week.

title: Forrester and G2 drive analyst mention uptick
anchor: Last 14 days analyst mentions: Forrester 3, G2 3, Gartner 1.
connect: Total analyst mentions reached 9 versus 6 in the prior 30 days.
body: Last 14 days analyst mentions: Forrester 3, G2 3, Gartner 1. Total analyst mentions reached 9 versus 6 in the prior 30 days.
trace: Analyst coverage acceleration driven primarily by Forrester and G2 activity in the recent two-week period.
